overall.description: A ship simulation game showing a first-person view from a vessel's bridge at night. The ship moves steadily through calm waters with illuminated navigation displays (radar, chart, and sonar) visible overhead, while the bow cuts through dark ocean swells.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with forward deck view over open ocean

events.description: small white vessel or buoy appears on starboard side in the distance

agents.ego_description: piloting the ship from the bridge, maintaining steady course through calm night waters while monitoring navigation displays

notable_agents.action: small vessel or navigation buoy visible at distance on starboard side, passed during transit

entered.detail: illuminated bow of the vessel with safety railings and deck markings visible in the ship's lights
three-screen bridge display showing radar sweep, nautical chart, and sonar depth readings
metal safety railing along the port side of the forecastle
metal safety railing along the starboard side of the forecastle
dark horizon line separating night sky from calm ocean waters

segments.scene_description: From the elevated bridge of a ship at night, the camera moves steadily forward over calm ocean waters. The illuminated foredeck stretches ahead with safety railings on both sides, while overhead navigation displays show rotating radar and chart data. A small distant vessel or buoy briefly appears on the starboard side around the 6-second mark before disappearing into the darkness as the ship maintains its course.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a steady forward progression from the elevated bridge of a ship, moving across a dark ocean landscape illuminated by onboard technology. It begins with a view over the foredeck, where safety railings frame the path forward and overhead navigation displays cast a glow with rotating radar and chart data. As the vessel maintains its course through the calm waters, the camera glides further into the night, momentarily revealing a small distant vessel or buoy on the starboard side. The scene then shifts as the distant light fades back into the surrounding darkness, leaving only the ship's steady advance across the open sea.
